title: quercus × cravenensis
intro: Quercus × cravenensis is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea, commonly known as English oak and sessile oak, respectively. The medicinal properties of Quercus × cravenensis lie in its bark, which contains a variety of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins with potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Quercus × cravenensis include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Quercus × cravenensis is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
